headlines.title: INSPECTION ASKED TO CUT INSURANCE
headlines.subtitle: Adoption of Anti - Wooden Shingle Clause Would Reduce Key Rate 9 Cents. SAVING TO THE INSURERS Incendiary Reward Will Lower Preimum in City $20,000 Per Year.
headlines.fullText: Application is now in for Fort Worth for an Inspection looking to a reduction in the insurance key rate. A 3 cents reduction is practically sure, and with the anti-wooden shingle clause in the building code a total of 9 cents reduction can be had. That, at present anomalies of insurance carried, would save the Insurers of Fort Worth $90,000 a year in premiums. A 2-cent cut Is expected on the showing and action is expected in a short time. Fire Chief Bideker has also asked City Attorney McCart to draft an Incendiary reward ordinance which will entitle Fort Worth to a further reduction. This incendiary reward provision entitles any city to the 2-cent cut that will offer a reward equivalent to $1 on every $1,000 worth of property as shown by the tax valuations At this rate Fort Worth would have to offer a reward of approximately $200 for conviction of incendiaries. The cut would amount to $20,000 a year in insurance premiums. The reward is open only to persons not connected with the city service. An effort to get the 3-cent cut for the building code with the antiwooden shingle clause was made over a year ago, but was defeated at that time on the claim that a sightly roof could not be had for less than about twice the cost of shingles. Since then Fire Chief Bideker has built a home on Jennings avenue using a galvanized iron shingle, and he claims the extra cost over wood shingles will be saved in what the insurance cut would amount to in five years. He also Invites an Inspection. Dallas now has in an application for Inspection for a reduced key rata. At present Dallas and Fort Worth have the same rate. 24 cents, but Dallas has a conflagration charge never made against Fort Worth. Oak Cliff, however, takes a rate in the thirties, while the North Side here has been allowed the same rate as the old town.

headlines.title: CITY TO KEEP DOWN WEEDS ALONG STREETS
headlines.subtitle: Prisoners and Men Hired for Purpose Will Do Work.
headlines.fullText: A general weed-cutting campaign was inaugurated by the city Wednesday morning, both city prisoners and men hired for the purpose being put to work in the fight against weeds that are higher than for years as a result of the recent heavy rains. One fleer under Jacks direction on the South Side and another under direction of Sanitation Officer Watson on the North Side. A third crew will start work Thursday and will cover the district between the Texas & Pacific railway and the Trinity river. The city weed cutters are cutting only weeds along sidewalks and alleys and will not go on any private property. Owners of all vacant lots are required to keep them free of weeds and the police department will enforce that ordinance. In carrying on the weed cutting campaign the same general plan as was followed in the fly campaign in April will he followed. An oiling campaign for pools of stagnant water was started several weeks ago. By means of the two it Is expected the number of mosquitos will be kept to a minimum.

headlines.title: SHRINERS LAY PLANS FOR BIG CEREMONY
headlines.subtitle: Glad Rags Ordered for Institution of Moslah Temple.
headlines.fullText: Fort Worth's streets will be a moving mass of red tops July 4, when caravans of Shriners from the four points of the Southwestern compass will pitch camps here for the institution of Moslah Temple. Glad tidings of the coming hosts were received Wednesday at a meeting held in the Chamber of Commerce rooms. The gathering of the Arabic clans will be the biggest ever held in Fort Worth. Hella Temple of Dallas will send a big patrol. Bedouin Temple of Oklahoma City will Institute the new temple in Fort Worth with Jack Jones, past potentate, as instituting officer. India Temple of Oklahoma City, El Karubah of Shreveport, La. will send a big caste, probably with a special train. Hella of Dallas will be here in large numbers and Nobles will make the trek from distant points over Texas, Oklahoma and Louisiana. It was decided Tuesday night that Moslah Temple shall pay the expenses of Hella's patrol and band for the instituting ceremony. Three hundred Moslah fezzes were ordered Tuesday night. The order for robes will be placed June 22.

headlines.title: "DOPED" AND ROBBED IS LABORER'S CLAIM
headlines.subtitle: Watauga Man Says Bartender Bought Drink Just Before He Lost Consciousness.
headlines.fullText: W. O. Mabry, a laborer for the Alvord Quarry and Construction Company at Watauga, told Judge Bardin in police court Wednesday morning that he was doped and robbed the night before. "I came to town Tuesday afternoon." he said. "I had a $20 bill, and walked into a saloon and bought a nickel glass of beer just to get it cashed. As I was walking out the bartender called me back and said, 'Let's have another'n.' I drank that one, and another one, and that was the last I remember. I don't know what he put in the beer, but It fixed me. I was robbed of $25 and a gold watch and chain." Mabry was released. Detectives are working on his case.

headlines.title: HARTSHORN IS NAMED AS GAME INSPECTOR
headlines.subtitle: Reports of Dynamiting Fish Cause Action-Will Watch Lake Worth.
headlines.fullText: A. I. Hartshorn, a local sportsman. has been appointed a deputy fish and game inspector for Tarrant and adjoining counties by Game, Fish and Oyster Commissioner W. G. Sterrett, at request of Police Commissioner Davis. The appointment was received by Commissioner Davis Wednesday. Commissioner Davis asked for the appointment as there have been numerous reports recently of dynamiting for fish along the Trinity river, which is now well stocked with fish as a result of the recent falls. Hartshorn also has been authorized to look after fish and birds at Lake Worth by the city commission.

headlines.title: B'NAI B'RITH PICKS M. MEHL PRESIDENT
headlines.subtitle: Other Officers Elected for Half Year. J. F. Shanblum, Secretary and David Carb, Treasurer.
headlines.fullText: B'Nai B'rith elected officers at a meeting Tuesday night. Meyer Mehl was elected president and the other officers were chosen as follows: Archie Salsberg, vice president; J. F. Shanblum, secretary: David Carb, treasurer; George Ginsburg, trustee, and Max Gens, trustee. Elections of B'Nai B'rith are semi-annual.

headlines.title: RISING WATER DELAYS WORK ON LAKE WORTH
headlines.subtitle: River Up Three Feet Since Tuesday-Job to Be Completed in Month.
headlines.fullText: Rising water in the west fork of the Trinity river has caused a postponement of the plan of the city to resume work on the Lake Worth reservoir Thursday. The river has risen three feet since the rains Tuesday and while no great amount of work remains to be done, the water will interfere with parts of the work remaining to be done. It is expected that as soon as the water recedes the work can be completed in a month. Practically no work has been done since the heavy rains of May.

headlines.title: CHILD, 3, IS KILLED
headlines.subtitle: Sister, 7, Playing With Gun When It Is Discharged.
headlines.fullText: SNYDER, Texas, June 17.-The 3-year-old son of J. W. Heatley, living twenty miles north of Snyder, was shot with a target rifle Monday evening and died Tuesday morning. His sister, 7, was playing with the gun. The bullet entered the chin and passed through, breaking the neck.
